perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Day Rate shall mean the daily rate at which Rubicon will provide services to ISA or ISA Clients. The Day Rate for each type of service provided will be as per the rate card set out in Exhibit C, such rates to be subject to annual review by the Parties on the anniversary of this Agreement provided that such rates may be reviewed on an ad hoc basis at any time should the Parties become aware of specific information which impacts on the validity or feasibility of the Day Rate.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Day Rate shall mean the rate at which Rubicon will provide services to ISA or ISA Clients.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the reference to Exhibit C and the mechanism for annual review of the Day Rate, the definition of 'Day Rate' becomes vague. Although 'Day Rate' is still mentioned, the method for determining the actual rate is removed, creating uncertainty. This omission creates a contradiction when coupled with section 4.1 (ISA Fees) and 4.2 (Rubicon Fees) which directly rely on the definition of the Day Rate and how it is determined from the rate card on Exhibit C.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 ISA Fees. In consideration of the services set forth in Section 2, ISA will sell or market the Rubicon Offerings at pre agreed list prices, and shall receive a commission on the gross revenues paid for the Rubicon Offerings less VAT if applicable of the Commission Rate; provided, however, that in the event that Rubicon proposes to charge at other than the Day Rate for the software development the Parties shall use all reasonable endeavours to agree an equitable adjustment to the Commission Rate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text omits the reference to Exhibit C and the mechanism for annual review of the Day Rate, which creates a contradiction. The contradicted text (4.1 ISA Fees) explicitly refers to the 'Day Rate' and implicitly to the 'Commission Rate' that depends on how the Day Rate is determined. By removing the definition of how to determine the Day Rate, the changed text creates a direct conflict, as it renders section 4.1 unenforceable since the Day Rate can no longer be determined based on Exhibit C, causing a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 Commission Rate shall mean the commission rate which will be used to calculate any amount which Rubicon will pay to ISA after the provision of Rubicon Offerings to ISA Clients as set forth in Exhibit D, such rates to be subject to annual review by the Parties on the anniversary of this Agreement provided that such rates may be reviewed on an ad hoc basis at any time should the Parties become aware of specific information which impacts on the validity or feasibility of the Commission Rate.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 Commission Rate shall mean the commission rate which will be used to calculate any amount which Rubicon will pay to ISA after the provision of Rubicon Offerings to ISA Clients.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to 'Exhibit D' and annual review process, the exact commission rate becomes undefined, although the concept of a 'Commission Rate' is retained. This omission creates a contradiction when referenced in section 4.1 (ISA Fees), as the method for calculating the amount using said 'Commission Rate' is no longer clearly defined, creating uncertainty and ambiguity in its enforecement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 ISA Fees. In consideration of the services set forth in Section 2, ISA will sell or market the Rubicon Offerings at pre agreed list prices, and shall receive a commission on the gross revenues paid for the Rubicon Offerings less VAT if applicable of the Commission Rate; provided, however, that in the event that Rubicon proposes to charge at other than the Day Rate for the software development the Parties shall use all reasonable endeavours to agree an equitable adjustment to the Commission Rate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the reference to Exhibit D and the annual review process for the Commission Rate, while the contradicted text in Section 4.1 relies on a defined 'Commission Rate' to calculate the commission. The omission creates ambiguity and uncertainty in the method for calculating the commission, directly contradicting the requirement in Section 4.1 for using the 'Commission Rate' for calculation. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2.1 As regards Intellectual Property created by Rubicon, Rubicon acknowledges and agrees that those Works, as defined below, shall belong exclusively to ISA subject to payment in accordance with clause 4.2. Works means, collectively, any work product (of any type), software, developments, processes, improvements, and all works of authorship, in whole or in part, whether patentable or not and whether copyrightable or not created as services provided directly to ISA or on behalf of ISA by Rubicon, which (i) are conceived or made by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents during the Term and relate directly to the business in which ISA and Rubicon(during the Term by ISA) are, had been or were proposing to be engaged in; or (ii) are conceived or made by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents during or after the Term and are made through the use of any ISA Confidential Information, or which result from any work performed by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents for ISA. It is agreed that the terms under which Rubicon agrees to work with ISA, including the Day Rate, reflect and will reflect any value or potential value of Intellectual Property created by Rubicon on behalf of ISA.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2.1 As regards Intellectual Property created by Rubicon, Rubicon acknowledges and agrees that those Works, as defined below, shall belong exclusively to ISA subject to payment in accordance with clause 4.2. Works means, collectively, any work product (of any type), software, developments, processes, improvements, and all works of authorship, in whole or in part, whether patentable or not and whether copyrightable or not created as services provided directly to ISA or on behalf of ISA by Rubicon, which (i) are conceived or made by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents during the Term and relate directly to the business in which ISA and Rubicon(during the Term by ISA) are, had been or were proposing to be engaged in; or (ii) are conceived or made by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents during or after the Term and are made through the use of any ISA Confidential Information, or which result from any work performed by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents for ISA.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence: 'It is agreed that the terms under which Rubicon agrees to work with ISA, including the Day Rate, reflect and will reflect any value or potential value of Intellectual Property created by Rubicon on behalf of ISA.', this creates uncertainty of ownership of the Intellectual Property that is being created and used. This sentence directly clarifies how Rubicon is being compensated for the Intellectual Property created and states that ISA owns the created property. Without this sentence it can be argued that Rubicon still owns the Intellectual property as the contract is missing the specific section that says how the Rubicon is giving it to ISA, since the original clause used 'Day Rate' as the value under which Rubicon agrees to give to ISA the Intellectual property
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2.1 As regards Intellectual Property created by Rubicon, Rubicon acknowledges and agrees that those Works, as defined below, shall belong exclusively to ISA subject to payment in accordance with clause 4.2. Works means, collectively, any work product (of any type), software, developments, processes, improvements, and all works of authorship, in whole or in part, whether patentable or not and whether copyrightable or not created as services provided directly to ISA or on behalf of ISA by Rubicon, which (i) are conceived or made by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents during the Term and relate directly to the business in which ISA and Rubicon(during the Term by ISA) are, had been or were proposing to be engaged in; or (ii) are conceived or made by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents during or after the Term and are made through the use of any ISA Confidential Information, or which result from any work performed by Rubicon, its employees, contractors, consultants or agents for ISA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the sentence clarifying that the 'Day Rate' reflects the value of intellectual property created by Rubicon and assigned to ISA. While the remaining text states that the 'Works' belong exclusively to ISA subject to payment in accordance with clause 4.2, the omission of the sentence creates an in-text contradiction, as it introduces ambiguity regarding the consideration given for the transfer of IP rights, potentially conflicting with the initial statement in 5.2.1: 'shall belong exclusively to ISA subject to payment in accordance with clause 4.2.' Without a clear statement on how Rubicon is compensated, ISA might need to provide more consideration.
